mapping.a_element: hydrodynamic interactions between sperm via flow fields created by beating tails
mapping.b_element: hydrodynamic interactions in swimming microorganisms at low Reynolds number

mapping.a_element: multi-particle collision dynamics (MPC) simulation method for fluid dynamics
mapping.b_element: resistive-force theory and slender-body theory for flagellar locomotion

mapping.a_element: synchronization and attraction of sperm due to hydrodynamic effects
mapping.b_element: hydrodynamic interactions leading to collective behavior in microorganisms

mapping.a_element: beating-phase difference and relative sperm position
mapping.b_element: kinematic requirements for net translation in swimming microorganisms

brief_justification: While both papers address hydrodynamic interactions at low Reynolds number, Paper A focuses on a specific simulation method (MPC) to study synchronization, attraction, and aggregation of sperm, whereas Paper B is a review of general biophysical principles and classical theoretical frameworks (e.g., resistive-force and slender-body theories). The shared hydrodynamic concepts are foundational but do not constitute the same underlying machinery or transferable process.
